problem.title: WebSocket Connection State Synchronization Failure
problem.description: Backend accepts WebSocket connections successfully but frontend UI persistently shows 'Disconnected' state
problem.manifestation: Ghost connection syndrome - functional backend connection with disconnected frontend UI state

primary.name: temporal_state_desynchronization
characteristics: UI state fails to reflect actual connection status
Event propagation chain breaks between connection and UI update

secondary.name: event_handler_timing_race
characteristics: Socket 'connect' event may fire before handlers are registered
State machine stuck in CONNECTING state
Connection validation logic has timing gaps

tertiary.name: context_provider_update_lag
characteristics: useMemo dependency array may not trigger updates
Complex connection state logic returns incorrect values
UI re-render doesn't reflect actual socket state

rootCause.technical: Complex multi-layer connection management abstraction creates state synchronization gaps
rootCause.architectural: Event-driven state management without atomic validation checkpoints
rootCause.temporal: Race conditions between socket connection establishment and React state updates

primary_issue.problem: Logic can return false when should return true - fails during ambiguous socket states

secondary_issue.problem: State check prevents handler execution if already CONNECTED, but UI may not reflect this

abstraction_layer.problem: Too many abstraction layers: useWebSocketSingleton → useConnectionManager → WebSocketConnectionManager creates state propagation delays

immediate.action: Fix connection state logic in WebSocketSingletonContext
immediate.implementation: Replace complex boolean logic with direct socket.connected check
immediate.code:
isConnecting: socket?.connecting === true,
isConnected: socket?.connected === true,

validation.implementation: Implement heartbeat validator that reconciles socket state with UI state
validation.code: useEffect(() => { const validator = setInterval(() => { if (socket?.connected && !isConnected) forceConnectedState(); }, 1000); }, [socket?.connected, isConnected]);

architecture.implementation: Reduce abstraction layers to minimize state propagation delay

temporal_patterns.lesson: WebSocket connections are inherently asynchronous - state management must account for timing gaps
temporal_patterns.application: Implement synchronous validation checkpoints with polling backup

event_driven_brittleness.lesson: Pure event-driven state management fails during timing edge cases
event_driven_brittleness.application: Combine event-driven with polling-based state validation for reliability

ui_state_reflection.lesson: React context updates don't guarantee immediate UI reflection during rapid state changes
ui_state_reflection.application: Use forced re-render triggers for critical connection state changes

abstraction_cost.lesson: Multiple abstraction layers in state management create synchronization delays
abstraction_cost.application: Keep connection state management as close to UI layer as possible

testScenarios.name: connection_state_mismatch_detection
testScenarios.steps: Start backend → Load frontend → Check logs vs UI state
testScenarios.expected: Backend connection logs match frontend UI state

testScenarios.name: rapid_connect_disconnect_sync
testScenarios.steps: Connect → Quick disconnect → Reconnect → Verify UI throughout
testScenarios.expected: UI reflects all state transitions accurately

testScenarios.name: network_interruption_recovery
testScenarios.steps: Establish connection → Simulate network drop → Restore network
testScenarios.expected: Automatic reconnection with proper state sync

preventionRules: Always validate WebSocket state changes with both event-driven and polling mechanisms
Minimize abstraction layers in real-time connection state management
Implement atomic state synchronization checkpoints for critical UI states
Use direct socket property checks rather than complex derived state logic
